ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn provides a generic cracker category; no specific onion-flavoured cracker entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers crackers and crispbreads as a category; no onion-specific cracker subcategory available. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for onion-flavoured crackers as a food product at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one individual pack of onion crackers, approximately 30 g, which is a typical single-serve snack portion as sold in a 3-pack format.

nutrient_source.title: Crackers, standard (plain salted), per 100 g — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18228)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No Singapore-specific onion cracker entry found in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain salted crackers (NDB 18228 / FDC ID 172023), which is the closest approved generic cracker reference. Onion flavouring contributes negligible macronutrient difference at typical seasoning levels. Sodium value adjusted upward slightly to 600 mg per 100 g to reflect typical onion-seasoned cracker formulations consistent with USDA Branded Food category data for flavoured crackers.

gi_source.title: Crackers, water biscuits and plain crackers — GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for onion-flavoured crackers sold in Singapore. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on plain wheat crackers and water biscuits reported in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 55–72 for wheat-based crackers). Onion seasoning does not materially alter the glycaemic response of the wheat-starch base. GL per serving calculated as (65 × 21.0 g available carbohydrate per 30 g serving) / 100 = 13.65, rounded to 13.7.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~600 mg per 100 g; ~180 mg per 30 g pack)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of onion crackers due to their elevated sodium content; a single 30 g pack provides approximately 180 mg sodium, and eating all three packs in one sitting would contribute ~540 mg sodium, representing roughly 24% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 65) and moderate glycemic load per serving (GL ~13.7)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Persons with Type 2 Diabetes should consume onion crackers in controlled portions, as the medium GI of 65 and a glycemic load of approximately 13.7 per 30 g pack can produce a moderate postprandial glucose rise; pairing with a protein or fibre source may help blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat with low saturated fat; high sodium and refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit onion cracker intake because the combination of high refined carbohydrate content, elevated sodium (~600 mg/100 g), and ultra-processed formulation is inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns recommended by the Singapore Heart Foundation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Onion Crackers (3-Pack) is a commercially produced savoury snack sold in Singapore, consisting of three individually wrapped packs of thin, baked wheat-flour crackers seasoned with onion flavouring. The crackers are crisp and light in texture with a savoury, mildly sweet onion taste. They are an ultra-processed snack product made primarily from refined wheat flour, vegetable oil, onion seasoning, salt, and leavening agents. Each pack weighs approximately 30 g, making the full 3-pack approximately 90 g total. The product is sold on field survey data and has been observed at Dapur Penyet - Funan. Nutritionally, onion crackers are energy-dense (~448 kcal per 100 g) and high in refined carbohydrates (~70 g per 100 g), with moderate protein (~9.5 g) and fat (~14 g). Sodium content is elevated at approximately 600 mg per 100 g, making portion control important for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), placing it in a category that requires moderation for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The product is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is likely halal, but contains gluten and is not suitable for those with wheat or gluten intolerance. It is commonly consumed as a between-meal snack or accompaniment to beverages in Singapore's multicultural food culture.
common_user_queries: How many calories in onion crackers?
Crackers 3 pack field survey data calories

confidence_reason: No exact entry for onion-flavoured crackers was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy generic plain salted cracker entry, which is the closest approved reference. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-based crackers. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172023, plain salted crackers) as no Singapore-specific onion cracker entry exists in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Sodium adjusted to 600 mg/100 g to reflect typical flavoured cracker formulations. 2. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on wheat cracker GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement for this specific product exists. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy reference for baked crackers with vegetable oil. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as product contains no animal-derived fat. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a baked grain product with no vitamin C source. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as product is plant-based with no B12 source. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as baked grain snacks contain no vitamin D. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference for plain crackers. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical commercial cracker formulations sold in Singapore; formal halal certification was not verified from the field survey listing. 6. Price of SGD 10.00 for a 3-pack is preserved from the input record (field survey data, Dapur Penyet - Funan). 7. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are used as closest-parent matches; no exact ontology code for onion-flavoured crackers exists in these systems.
